rulings.Case Title: THERESA LYNNE MURPHY VS. SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY ASSESOR-RECORDER, PHIL TING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Theresa Murphy Notice Of Motion For, Order To Show Cause Re Contempt For The Willful Disobedience Of Lawful Judgment Quieting Title; Statement Of Facts In Re Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2018, Line 2, Plaintiff Theresa Murphy'S Notice Of Motion For, Order To Show Cause Re Contempt For The Willful Disobedience Of Lawful Judgment Quieting Title; Statement Of Facts In Re Contempt. Plaintiff Theresa Murphy's amended motion for an order to show cause re contempt is denied. "It is well established that the affidavit by which a contempt proceeding is instituted, in order to sufficiently support an adjudication of contempt, must state facts constituting the offense, otherwise the court is without jurisdiction, and the facts essential to establish jurisdiction are (1) the making of the order, (2) knowledge of the order, (3) ability of the respondent to render compliance, and (4) willful disobedience of the order." (People v. Sup. Crt. (1965) 239 Cal.App.2d 99, 104.) Here, Ms. Murphy fails to show by a preponderance of the evidence, much less beyond a reasonable doubt, that The Bank of New York Mellon is willfully violating the judgment filed January 27, 2012. Ms. Murphy contends that the Bank has no interest in the property located at11 High Street, #201 and it is engaging in improper conduct by asserting an ownership interest over the property. The January 27, 2012 judgment determined the status of title as of December 29, 2005. (Bank's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 10 (Murphy's Notice of Motion for Summary Judgment).) The evidence indicates that MERS subsequently transferred the deed of trust on the property to the Bank on October 10, 2012. There is insufficient evidence to establish that this transfer was fraudulent, void, and/or unenforceable. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to find that Bank has no interest in the property and that it has been acting in contempt of the January 27, 2012 judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the Bank is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA Q. RODRIGUEZ VS. DORA E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition, For Monetary And Evidentiary Sanctions, And For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2018, Line 4, Defendant Dora Rodriguez' Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Deposition, For Monetary And Evidentiary Sanctions, And For A Protective Order. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is transferred to the 9:30 a.m. calendar in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TERRENCE KRISTOFF ET AL VS. SHEILA MAUREEN PERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2018, Line 14, Defendants PV Holding Corp. and Sheila Perry's motion to compel. Defendants PV Holding Corp. and Sheila Perry's motion to compel further answers to deposition questions of deponents Miguel Diaz and Wai Ng is granted. Defendant City and County of San Francisco has not shown that either Mr. Diaz or Mr. Ng has a protecible privacy interest in information about prior similar accidents that supports either an instruction not to answer on privacy grounds or requires moving defendants to show that prior similar accidents are directly relevant to the issues in this case. Absent such a privacy interest, the instructions not to answer questions were erroneous and thus the City is required to make Mr. Diaz and Mr. Ng available for further sessions of their depositions where they are required to answer questions seeking information about prior similar accidents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN ELAINE GOODRICH MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Medical Board of California's demurrer to the single cause of action for rescission in the complaint filed by Plaintiff Karen Goodrich is sustained without leave to amend. Dr. Goodrich's effort to seek judicial review of the Medical Board's decision effective March 10, 2014 adopting the stipulated settlement and disciplinary order is time-barred. In order to timely seek judicial review of that decision, Dr. Goodrich was required to file a petition for a writ of mandamus within 30 days after March 10, 2014. Dr. Goodrich's suspension was terminated in June 2014, she is currently still in good standing, and allowed to practice medicine. The public records regarding the 2012 accusation and the subsequent order cannot be expunged because the Medical Board is required to publically disclose such information by law. (Business and Professions Code 803.1(a)(3) and 2027(b)(2); California Code of Regulations 1354.5(b).) To the extent Dr. Goodrich contends that the order should be expunged because the underlying accusation by Sierra Vista Medical Center lacks sufficient evidence, this argument is barred by collateral estoppel because the court in San Luis Obispo denied her writ concerning this issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Medical Board is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HUNTER HANNOCK ET AL VS. CLEVER MACHINE, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 18, 2018, Line 13, PLAINTIFF HUNTER HANNOCK, COLLEEN HANCOCK'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment. Plaintiffs Hunter and Colleen Hancock's application for a right to attach order and a writ of attachment is granted in substantial part. The Hancocks are entitled to a right to attach order and writ of attachment in the amount of $265,012.90 on the condition that they file an undertaking in the amount of $10,000. The money in defendant Clever Machine, LLC's bank account is and is likely to remain the only asset Clever Machine has to satisfy the arbitration award in favor of the Hancocks. The liquidity of this asset, the unilateral power of Clever Machine's CEO to dispose of the asset, and Clever Machine's rejection of the Hancocks' request to pay the amount of the award issued on March 6, 2018 are sufficient to show that there is a danger that the asset will not be available and the arbitration award will be rendered ineffectual absent the grant of provisional relief. Granting provisional relief will not invade the arbitrator's province because the arbitrator has already issued his award on the Hancocks' contractual claim for $196, 831.49 as well as provided a method by which to calculate an award of prejudgment interest. The amount of the attachment is calculated as follows: $196, 831.49 for the arbitration award on the contract claim; $ 45, 751.92 for estimated prejudgment interest; and $22, 429.49 for JAMS arbitration fees. All other amounts sought to be included in the attachment are neither fixed nor readily ascertainable at this point. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Hancocks is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
